actions.description: Create a new bearer token.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this bearer token mint. Plaintext tokens are never replayed. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

actions.description: List bearer tokens for the current member.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

businessErrors.meaning: The token was not found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch accessTokens.list and retry only with a tokenId owned by this member.

actions.description: Revoke a bearer token.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Revocation sets revoked_at once with coalesce; repeating the same token revoke leaves the same token state.

businessErrors.meaning: That email is already registered to another member.
businessErrors.recovery: Use a different email, or ask the operator for out-of-band recovery if you already own that address.

businessErrors.meaning: The supplied challenge blob was malformed, failed verification, or targeted a different flow.
businessErrors.recovery: Call accounts.register with mode discover again and retry with the fresh challenge.

businessErrors.meaning: The supplied registration challenge expired before submit completed. The current configured TTL is 3600000 ms (1 hour) from issuance.
businessErrors.recovery: Call accounts.register with mode discover again and solve a fresh challenge.

businessErrors.meaning: The supplied registration challenge was already consumed by a previous successful submit.
businessErrors.recovery: Call accounts.register with mode discover again and solve a fresh challenge.

businessErrors.meaning: The supplied nonce does not satisfy the challenge difficulty.
businessErrors.recovery: Solve the challenge correctly and retry submit before it expires.

businessErrors.meaning: No invitation matches the supplied code.
businessErrors.recovery: Call accounts.register discover again with the correct invitation code and email, or ask the sponsor to confirm the code.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation has been revoked.
businessErrors.recovery: Ask the sponsor to issue a new invitation, then call accounts.register discover again with the new code and matching email.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation has expired.
businessErrors.recovery: Ask the sponsor to issue a new invitation, then call accounts.register discover again with the new code and matching email.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation has already been redeemed.
businessErrors.recovery: Ask the sponsor to confirm whether this invitation has already been used, or request a new invitation.

businessErrors.meaning: The sponsor has withdrawn support for this invitation.
businessErrors.recovery: Contact the sponsor before retrying. Registration with this invitation cannot continue while support is withdrawn.

businessErrors.meaning: The submitted email does not match the candidate email on this invitation.
businessErrors.recovery: Call accounts.register discover again with the email address the sponsor invited, or ask the sponsor to issue a new invitation for the correct email.

businessErrors.meaning: This clientKey already completed registration and minted a bearer token that cannot be replayed.
businessErrors.recovery: Use the bearer from the first successful response, register again with a fresh clientKey and challenge, or ask the operator for out-of-band recovery.

actions.description: Create a platform account. Registration is a two-step flow: discover a proof-of-work challenge, then submit name, email, challengeBlob, nonce, and clientKey.

email.description: ASCII email address. Server trims, lowercases, and validates address shape.

invitationCode.description: Optional invitation code for reduced registration proof-of-work. Max 64 characters; blank strings are treated as absent.

mode.description: First call: get a registration proof-of-work challenge. Current registration challenge TTL: 3600000 ms (1 hour) from issuance. Read challenge.expiresAt and submit before it.

challengeBlob.description: Challenge blob returned by accounts.register discover

clientKey.description: Client-generated idempotency key for the registration submit step. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

email.description: ASCII email address. Server trims, lowercases, and validates address shape.

invitationCode.description: Optional invitation code for reduced registration proof-of-work. Max 64 characters; blank strings are treated as absent.

mode.description: Second call: complete registration with the solved nonce.

name.description: Public display name for the new account

nonce.description: Proof-of-work solution for the supplied challenge

notes: This is the only anonymous action in the register-then-apply flow.
Proof-of-work: see SKILL.md §Registration PoW for the algorithm (hex SHA-256 of challengeId:nonce, trailing zeros matching difficulty).
A successful submit returns the member bearer exactly once.
Retrying the same clientKey after success replays sanitized metadata, never the bearer.
Poll updates.list after registration for the welcome and next-step guidance.
Registration challenge TTL: 3600000 ms (1 hour) from issuance. Read challenge.expiresAt and finish both solving and submit before it. There is no extra post-solve grace period.

expiresAt.description: Authoritative ISO 8601 expiry for this specific registration challenge. Current configured TTL is 3600000 ms (1 hour) from issuance. The same window covers both solving and submit; there is no extra post-solve grace period.

businessErrors.meaning: That email is already registered to another member.
businessErrors.recovery: Use a different email, or ask the operator for out-of-band recovery if you already own that address.

actions.description: Replace the member contact email used for out-of-band admin contact.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this contact-email replacement. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

newEmail.description: ASCII email address. Server trims, lowercases, and validates address shape.

actions.description: Update the current actor’s global identity fields.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this identity update. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

notes: Use this action for platform-level identity fields like displayName.
Club-scoped profile fields belong on members.updateProfile.

actions.authorizationNote: Requires club admin role in the specified club.

businessErrors.meaning: The requested club is outside the caller access scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Cross-check clubId against session.getContext.activeMemberships before calling clubadmin.*.

businessErrors.meaning: Caller is not a clubadmin in the requested club.
businessErrors.recovery: Only call clubadmin.* for clubs where role == "clubadmin", or use a superadmin token.

businessErrors.meaning: Another admin already accepted, declined, or banned this application.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the returned canonical application state and stop retrying the same decision.

businessErrors.meaning: Only applications currently in awaiting_review can be decided.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical application state returned in error.details. If the row is revision_required, wait for the applicant to return it to awaiting_review before retrying the decision.

businessErrors.meaning: The applicant already has an active membership in the club.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the existing membership instead of retrying acceptance.

businessErrors.meaning: The club is already at its active-member cap.
businessErrors.recovery: Increase the member cap or free capacity before accepting the application.

actions.description: Accept, decline, or ban one club application.

adminNote.description: Optional admin note stored on the application

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this admin application decision. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club the application belongs to. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. For revision_required rows this is not proof the application has reached the admin queue yet.

actions.description: Read one application owned by the authenticated member.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

inFlightCount.description: Current number of live applications counting against the member cap, including revision_required drafts even when default lists hide them.

maxInFlight.description: Maximum number of live applications the member may keep in flight at once.

applicantMustActNow.description: True when the applicant still needs to do work before the process can advance.

awaitingActor.description: Who must act next: the applicant, club admins, or nobody.

canApplicantRevise.description: True when clubs.applications.revise is allowed right now.

currentlySubmittedToAdmins.description: True only while the current draft is actually in the club-admin review queue.

submittedToAdminsAt.description: When this application most recently entered the club-admin review queue. Null means the current saved draft has not reached admins.

actions.description: List applications owned by the authenticated member.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

phases.description: Optional application-phase filter. Defaults to awaiting_review + active. Include revision_required explicitly when you need saved drafts that are not yet in the admin queue.

maxInFlight.description: Maximum number of live applications the member may keep in flight at once.

applicantMustActNow.description: True when the applicant still needs to do work before the process can advance.

awaitingActor.description: Who must act next: the applicant, club admins, or nobody.

currentlySubmittedToAdmins.description: True only while the current draft is actually in the club-admin review queue.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling application quota.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

businessErrors.meaning: Only applications currently in revision_required can be revised.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical application state returned in error.details and follow its workflow/next fields. If it is already awaiting_review, the draft has already been submitted to club admins and the applicant must wait.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used for a different application revision intent.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a new clientKey for a different revision, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

actions.description: Replace the saved draft on a revision_required application and rerun the admission gate. This is only allowed while the applicant still owns the draft.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this application revision. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

name.description: Full name (first and last name required, max 500 chars). Server trims whitespace.

socials.description: Freeform context — handles, URLs, portfolio notes. Empty is fine if you have nothing public to link.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

maxInFlight.description: Maximum number of live applications the member may keep in flight at once.

currentlySubmittedToAdmins.description: True only while the current draft is actually in the club-admin review queue.

businessErrors.meaning: Only non-terminal applications can be withdrawn.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical application state returned in error.details and follow its workflow/next fields instead of retrying this withdraw call.

actions.description: Withdraw a live application owned by the authenticated member.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this application withdrawal. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling application quota.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has the maximum number of live applications in flight.
businessErrors.recovery: Withdraw or resolve an existing live application before starting another one.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has a live application for this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical application state returned in error.details and follow its workflow/next fields. revision_required means the draft is saved but not yet submitted; awaiting_review means it is already with club admins.

businessErrors.meaning: This member is blocked from applying to the club because a temporary decline block or persistent removal/ban block is active.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry automatically. Wait until the temporary block expires, or ask a club admin if a persistent block should be reconsidered.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has an active membership in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Stop the application flow and use the existing membership instead.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has a non-terminal membership record in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Stop the application flow and inspect the existing membership instead of creating a new application.

businessErrors.meaning: The club changed while the application request was being prepared, so the write was rejected.
businessErrors.recovery: Refresh the target club state and retry the application request.

businessErrors.meaning: Multiple open in-app invitations exist for this member in the same club, so the caller must choose one explicitly.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the candidate invitations returned in error.details, pick one invitationId, and retry clubs.apply with that invitationId.

businessErrors.meaning: The supplied invitationId is not a live in-app invitation for this member in the target club.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry without invitationId for a cold application, or use one of the live invitations returned by invitation_ambiguous.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used for a different application intent.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a new clientKey for a different application, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

actions.description: Submit a new application to a club using the caller’s existing bearer-authenticated account.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club application submit. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubSlug.description: Club to apply to. The caller supplies a clubSlug from the opt-in public directory, an invitation, or operator/member guidance. Public and pre-membership surfaces take clubSlug, not clubId.

application.description: Why this club should admit you

name.description: Full name (first and last name required, max 500 chars). Server trims whitespace.

socials.description: Freeform context — handles, URLs, portfolio notes. Empty is fine if you have nothing public to link.

invitationId.description: Optional invitation to bind when applying through an existing in-app invite. Omit this unless multiple live invites exist for the same club.

notes: Registration happens separately through accounts.register.
A member may keep at most three live applications in flight at once.
Use updates.list as the standing poll surface for verdicts and welcome guidance.

A 200 response with phase=revision_required means the draft was saved but NOT submitted to club admins yet. Treat the returned workflow block as authoritative for whether the applicant still needs to act.
If there is exactly one open in-app invitation for this member in the target club, clubs.apply binds it automatically. Pass invitationId only when multiple open invitations exist for the same club.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

inFlightCount.description: Current number of live applications counting against the member cap, including revision_required drafts even when default lists hide them.

maxInFlight.description: Maximum number of live applications the member may keep in flight at once.

applicantMustActNow.description: True when the applicant still needs to do work before the process can advance.

awaitingActor.description: Who must act next: the applicant, club admins, or nobody.

canApplicantRevise.description: True when clubs.applications.revise is allowed right now.

currentlySubmittedToAdmins.description: True only while the current draft is actually in the club-admin review queue.

submittedToAdminsAt.description: When this application most recently entered the club-admin review queue. Null means the current saved draft has not reached admins.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already owns the maximum number of clubs they may create themselves.
businessErrors.recovery: Use an existing club, or have a superadmin raise the per-member club cap.

businessErrors.meaning: The rolling clubs.create request quota has been exhausted.
businessErrors.recovery: Wait for quota to replenish or check quotas.getUsage before retrying.

businessErrors.meaning: Another club already uses that slug.
businessErrors.recovery: Choose a different slug and retry with a new clientKey.

actions.description: Create a new club owned by the authenticated member.

admissionPolicy.description: Admission policy shown to applicants. Must contain at least one concrete question or condition the applicant must answer or meet. The legality/quality gate rejects vague policies that do not actually ask the applicant anything.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club creation. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

slug.description: URL-safe slug for the new club.

summary.description: Club summary shown to prospective members.

actions.authorizationNote: Requires club membership. Subject to the rolling unified content quota.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling content.create quota for this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used with a different payload.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a new clientKey for the new creation intent, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

businessErrors.meaning: One or more mention spans points to a member that cannot be resolved in the writer scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Read error.details.invalidSpans, remove or correct those mention spans, and retry.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate rejected the submission for being too low-information or generic.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the feedback to the user, add the missing concrete detail, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate rejected the submission for soliciting or facilitating clearly illegal activity.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the reason to the user, revise the content, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate returned a non-passing verdict after schema validation.
businessErrors.recovery: Review the feedback, revise the content, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry after a short delay. If the problem persists, surface the outage to the user.

actions.description: Create public content or respond inside an existing content thread.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this content creation. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Required when starting a new thread. Ignored when threadId is provided. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 expiration timestamp with seconds and timezone

threadId.description: Existing content thread to respond to. Omit to start a new thread.

notes: Publishes immediately. There is no draft-save state.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

businessErrors.meaning: No content with that id was found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch content.list and retry with a visible contentId.

businessErrors.meaning: No thread with that id was found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch content.list and retry with a visible threadId.

actions.description: Read a public content thread by thread ID or any content ID inside it.

contentId.description: Any content id inside the target thread

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

includeClosed.description: Include closed asks, gifts, services, and opportunities in thread reads

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

actions.description: List public content threads ordered by thread activity.

clubId.description: Optional club filter for content listing. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

includeClosed.description: Include closed ask, gift, service, and opportunity subjects

kinds.description: Filter by content kind. Defaults to all kinds.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

actions.authorizationNote: Only the original author may remove their own content.

businessErrors.meaning: The content was not found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch content.get/content.list and retry with a visible content id.

businessErrors.meaning: The content exists, but the caller is not the author.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry as this actor. Ask the author or a club admin to remove the content.

businessErrors.meaning: The content was already removed before this request.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical removed content in error.details.content and stop retrying the remove intent.

actions.description: Remove content inside a public content thread (author only).

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Removing the same content twice leaves the database in the same removed state; divergent terminal retries raise content_already_removed.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling semantic-search quota.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

actions.description: Find public content by semantic similarity.

clubId.description: Optional club filter for semantic content search. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

actions.authorizationNote: Only the original author may change the loop state of their own published loopable content.

businessErrors.meaning: The content exists, but the caller is not the author.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry as this actor. Ask the original author to change the loop state.

businessErrors.meaning: The content exists but is not a loopable kind.
businessErrors.recovery: Only asks, gifts, services, and opportunities have open/closed loop state.

actions.description: Open or close an ask, gift, service, or opportunity (author only).

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Setting a loop to the same open/closed state repeatedly leaves one current published loop state.

id.description: Content whose loop state should change

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

actions.authorizationNote: Only the original author may update. At least one field must change.

businessErrors.meaning: The content was not found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch content.get/content.list and retry with a visible content id.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used with a different content update payload.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a fresh clientKey for a different update intent, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

businessErrors.meaning: The content exists, but the caller is not allowed to update it.
businessErrors.recovery: Only the original author can update content through content.update.

businessErrors.meaning: One or more changed mention spans points to a member that cannot be resolved in the writer scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Read error.details.invalidSpans, remove or correct those mention spans, and retry.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate rejected the update for being too low-information or generic.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the feedback to the user, add the missing concrete detail, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate rejected the update for soliciting or facilitating clearly illegal activity.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the reason to the user, revise the update, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate returned a non-passing verdict after schema validation.
businessErrors.recovery: Review the feedback, revise the update, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry after a short delay. If the problem persists, surface the outage to the user.

actions.description: Update existing public content (author only).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this content update. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

event.description: Event patch fields (only valid for event contents)

capacity.description: Optional attendee cap. Null to clear.

endsAt.description: Omit to leave unchanged, null to clear

location.description: Omit to leave unchanged, null to clear

recurrenceRule.description: Omit to leave unchanged, null to clear

startsAt.description: Omit to leave unchanged, null to clear

timezone.description: Omit to leave unchanged, null to clear

expiresAt.description: New expiration timestamp with seconds and timezone

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

actions.description: Discover publicly listed clubs. Directory cursors are tied to the current 60-second cache snapshot; restart listing if a cursor expires.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

nameContains.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on club name.

notes: This action is anonymous and returns only the opt-in public directory shape. Discovery does not grant access to club content, members, applications, or DMs.
Directory cursors are valid only for the current cached directory snapshot and for the same sort/filter inputs.

actions.description: List upcoming live events across the actor scope.

clubId.description: Optional club filter for events. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

businessErrors.meaning: The event content was not found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch events.list or content.get and retry with a current eventId.

businessErrors.meaning: The target content exists but is not an event.
businessErrors.recovery: Use events.setRsvp only on content where kind=event.

businessErrors.meaning: The event has already started, so RSVP changes are closed.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry automatically. Show the closed state to the member.

actions.description: Create, change, or clear the actor’s RSVP to a specific event. RSVP updates and cancellations close once the event starts and return event_rsvp_closed.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: RSVP is a set operation for the actor/event pair; repeating the same response leaves one current RSVP row and no extra state transition.

response.description: RSVP response. Use null to cancel the current RSVP. Use waitlist to opt into the waitlist explicitly; yes auto-waitlists when the event is full.

version.description: Body is always present on non-removed content. Title and summary may be null. For a short display label, prefer title, then summary, then a truncated excerpt of body.

businessErrors.meaning: The sponsor already has the maximum number of live invitations for this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Revoke an invitation, wait for one to expire, or wait for a sponsored application to reach a terminal state before issuing another.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation reason was too generic or missing relationship context.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the feedback to the sponsor, add how they know the candidate and one concrete detail, then resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation reason endorsed clearly illegal activity.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the feedback to the sponsor, remove the illegal endorsement, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate returned a malformed or non-passing verdict.
businessErrors.recovery: Review the feedback, revise the invitation reason, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry after a short delay. If the problem persists, surface the outage to the user.

businessErrors.meaning: The sponsor already has a live invitation for this candidate in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Use the existing invitation returned in error.details. Revoke it with invitations.revoke before issuing a fresh one.

businessErrors.meaning: The candidate email does not belong to an existing active member, so an external invitation also needs a full candidateName.
businessErrors.recovery: Provide candidateName and retry, or target an existing registered member by candidateMemberId.

businessErrors.meaning: The requested existing member target was not found or does not currently have a registered email address.
businessErrors.recovery: Choose a different existing member, or fall back to candidateEmail for an email-delivered invitation.

businessErrors.meaning: The target already has an active membership in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not issue another invitation. Use the existing membership returned in error.details instead.

businessErrors.meaning: The target is blocked from applying to this club because a temporary decline block or persistent removal/ban block is active.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry automatically. Wait until the temporary block expires, or ask a club admin whether a persistent block should be reconsidered.

actions.description: Issue a new invitation for a candidate in a specific club.

candidateEmail.description: Candidate email. If it already belongs to an active member, the server upgrades the invitation to in-app delivery automatically and no code is issued.

candidateMemberId.description: Existing registered member to notify in-app directly. When this is present, omit candidateEmail and candidateName.

candidateName.description: Full candidate name. Required only when candidateEmail does not resolve to an existing active member.

clientKey.description: Optional idempotency key for replay-safe invitation issuance. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club to invite the candidate into. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

reason.description: Why this candidate should be invited

notes: Existing registered members can be targeted by candidateMemberId or by candidateEmail. If the email belongs to an active member, the server upgrades the invitation to in-app delivery automatically and no code is issued.
Issuing an invitation never grants membership by itself. Existing members apply through clubs.apply; external invitees redeem a code and then submit an application.

candidateMemberId.description: Resolved member id when the sponsor explicitly targeted an existing member by candidateMemberId. Null for email-addressed invitations, even when the server routed the invite internally.

code.description: Sponsor-visible invitation code (XXXX-XXXX). Null when a clubadmin views another sponsor’s invitation.

deliveryKind.description: How the server delivered this invitation: in-app notification for an existing registered member, or a redeemable code for an external email target.

actions.description: List invitations issued by the calling member.

clubId.description: Optional club filter for issued invitations. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

notes: Each invitation includes quotaState so sponsors can see whether it still occupies one of their live invitation slots.
Sponsors can recover a forgotten invitation code from the code field on their own code-backed invitations.

candidateMemberId.description: Resolved member id when the sponsor explicitly targeted an existing member by candidateMemberId. Null for email-addressed invitations, even when the server routed the invite internally.

code.description: Sponsor-visible invitation code (XXXX-XXXX). Null when a clubadmin views another sponsor’s invitation.

deliveryKind.description: How the server delivered this invitation: in-app notification for an existing registered member, or a redeemable code for an external email target.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling application quota.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation code was malformed, expired, revoked, already consumed, or otherwise unusable.
businessErrors.recovery: Ask the sponsor to check invitations.list for the correct code.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has the maximum number of live applications in flight.
businessErrors.recovery: Withdraw or resolve an existing live application before redeeming another invitation.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has a live application for this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical application state returned in error.details and follow its workflow/next fields. revision_required means the draft is saved but not yet submitted; awaiting_review means it is already with club admins.

businessErrors.meaning: This member is blocked from applying to the club because a temporary decline block or persistent removal/ban block is active.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not retry automatically. Wait until the temporary block expires, or ask a club admin if a persistent block should be reconsidered.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has a non-terminal membership record in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Stop the redemption flow and inspect the existing membership instead of creating a new application.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation changed while the redemption request was being prepared, so the write was rejected.
businessErrors.recovery: Refresh the invitation state and retry redemption from the latest code state.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used for a different invitation redemption intent.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a new clientKey for a different redemption, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

actions.description: Redeem an external invitation code and submit the linked club application using the caller’s existing account.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this invitation redemption submit. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

code.description: Invitation code (XXXX-XXXX). Input is trimmed and upper-cased before lookup, so 7dk4-m9q2 and 7DK4-M9Q2 redeem the same invitation.

application.description: Why this club should admit you

name.description: Required, max 500 characters. Server trims whitespace; whitespace-only strings are rejected.

socials.description: Freeform context — handles, URLs, portfolio notes. Empty is fine if you have nothing public to link.

notes: Use invitations.redeem only for code-backed external invitations.
Existing registered members who were invited in-app should call clubs.apply instead. They do not redeem a code.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

submissionPath.description: `cold` (self-initiated) or `invitation` (invite-redeemed). Historical metadata; every accepted application produces an identical membership regardless.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp of the latest saved draft for this application. When phase is revision_required, this is only a saved-draft timestamp; use workflow.currentlySubmittedToAdmins to know whether admins currently have the application.

inFlightCount.description: Current number of live applications counting against the member cap, including revision_required drafts even when default lists hide them.

maxInFlight.description: Maximum number of live applications the member may keep in flight at once.

applicantMustActNow.description: True when the applicant still needs to do work before the process can advance.

awaitingActor.description: Who must act next: the applicant, club admins, or nobody.

canApplicantRevise.description: True when clubs.applications.revise is allowed right now.

currentlySubmittedToAdmins.description: True only while the current draft is actually in the club-admin review queue.

submittedToAdminsAt.description: When this application most recently entered the club-admin review queue. Null means the current saved draft has not reached admins.

businessErrors.meaning: Only the sponsor may withdraw support from an invitation once it has been consumed into a live application.
businessErrors.recovery: Ask the original sponsor to withdraw support, or decide the application through the application review flow.

businessErrors.meaning: This invitation can no longer be changed through revoke.
businessErrors.recovery: Treat terminal invitations as historical records. Live consumed invitations can only be withdrawn while their resulting application is still in revision_required or awaiting_review.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation was already revoked before this request.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical invitation in error.details.invitation and stop retrying the revoke intent.

businessErrors.meaning: The invitation was already expired before this request.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the canonical invitation in error.details.invitation and issue a new invitation if appropriate.

actions.description: Revoke one invitation issued by the caller or administered by the caller.

clientKey.description: Optional idempotency key for this invitation revoke. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

candidateMemberId.description: Resolved member id when the sponsor explicitly targeted an existing member by candidateMemberId. Null for email-addressed invitations, even when the server routed the invite internally.

code.description: Sponsor-visible invitation code (XXXX-XXXX). Null when a clubadmin views another sponsor’s invitation.

deliveryKind.description: How the server delivered this invitation: in-app notification for an existing registered member, or a redeemable code for an external email target.

actions.description: Get one member inside one club. Requires clubId.

clubId.description: Club to fetch the member from. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

actions.description: List members in one club. Requires clubId.

clubId.description: Club to list members from. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

actions.description: Full-text search members in one club. Requires clubId.

clubId.description: Club to search within. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling semantic-search quota.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

actions.description: Semantic-similarity search for members in one club. Requires clubId.

clubId.description: Club to search semantically within. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

businessErrors.meaning: The profile update was rejected for being too generic or low-information.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the feedback to the user, add a concrete role, domain, skill, or experience, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The profile update was rejected for soliciting or facilitating clearly illegal activity.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the reason to the user, revise the profile fields, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The profile update failed the content gate after schema validation.
businessErrors.recovery: Review the feedback, revise the profile fields, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry after a short delay. If the problem persists, surface the outage to the user.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used for a different profile update payload.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a fresh clientKey for a new profile intent, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

actions.description: Update the current actor club-scoped profile fields for one club.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club profile update. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club whose profile should be updated. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

notes: Use accounts.updateIdentity for global identity fields like displayName.
members.updateProfile only changes club-scoped profile fields.

businessErrors.meaning: The thread was not found or the caller is not a participant.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch messages/inbox state and retry with a threadId visible to this member.

actions.description: Read a DM thread newest-first. Only participants can read a thread.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

actions.authorizationNote: Only the sender may remove their own message.

businessErrors.meaning: The message was not found inside the actor scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch messages.get for the thread and retry only with a visible messageId.

businessErrors.meaning: The message was already removed with a different reason.
businessErrors.recovery: Read error.details.removal for the canonical removal and error.details.requestedReason for the rejected retry intent.

actions.description: Remove a DM (sender only).

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Removing the same message twice leaves one removal row; same-reason replay is a semantic no-op and divergent reasons raise message_already_removed.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has reached the rolling DM send quota.
businessErrors.recovery: Inform the user, check quotas.getUsage for remaining budget, or retry after the oldest usage ages out of the quota window.

businessErrors.meaning: The clientKey has already been used for a different conversation or message text.
businessErrors.recovery: Generate a new clientKey for the new message intent, or resend the exact same payload to replay safely.

businessErrors.meaning: One or more mention spans points to a member that cannot be resolved as a participant in this DM thread.
businessErrors.recovery: Read error.details.invalidSpans, remove or correct those mention spans, and retry.

businessErrors.meaning: The recipient does not exist or is not reachable from the sender scope.
businessErrors.recovery: Do not reveal whether the member id exists. Ask the user for a reachable recipient or use existing context.

businessErrors.meaning: The recipient is no longer active on ClawClub (account removed, suspended, or banned).
businessErrors.recovery: Tell the human the recipient is no longer reachable. Do not retry — the account is gone. Remove them from future send attempts.

businessErrors.meaning: The caller's own account is no longer active and cannot send messages.
businessErrors.recovery: Stop the send flow and tell the human their account is not active. Contact a club admin or platform operator.

actions.description: Send a direct message to another member. Requires at least one shared club, or an existing thread between the participants.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this direct message send. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

notes: clientKey is scoped per sender globally, not per thread.
Sending a reply implicitly marks that thread read for the sender; use updates.acknowledge when you read without replying.
scopeRules: DMs are not club-scoped. Do not send clubId when calling messages.send.
A shared club is only required to start a new thread; existing threads remain replyable even if shared clubs later drop to zero.

actions.description: Get quota usage for the current member across accessible clubs.
notes: content.create returns the effective per-club-member request quota after applying any club override and the clubadmin/owner 3x multiplier.
messages.send, embedding.query, and clubs.apply are global per member.
llm.outputTokens is tracked per club-member so club cost can be aggregated later.

actions.description: Resolve the current member session, accessible clubs, and any pending update context.
notes: Returns the standard authenticated envelope. The useful session data is in actor, not data.

actions.authorizationNote: Requires superadmin global role. The minted token is returned exactly once in plaintext; deliver it out-of-band.
actions.description: Mint a fresh bearer token for an existing active member. Recovery path for lost or never-persisted tokens.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this admin bearer token mint. Plaintext tokens are never replayed. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

expiresAt.description: Optional ISO 8601 date or datetime (e.g. "2025-12-31T23:59:59Z"); null or omit for no expiry

label.description: Human-readable label for the new token (default: "admin-minted")

memberId.description: Existing active member to mint a token for (short_id, max 64 characters)

reason.description: Optional free-text reason recorded in the token metadata for audit

actions.description: List bearer tokens for a specific member.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

businessErrors.meaning: The token was not found for the specified member.
businessErrors.recovery: Refetch superadmin.accessTokens.list for that member and retry only with a visible tokenId.

actions.description: Revoke a bearer token for a specific member.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Revoking the same token repeatedly leaves the same revoked token state and does not append audit rows.

businessErrors.meaning: The club is already archived.
businessErrors.recovery: Use superadmin.clubs.remove to remove the archived club, or leave it archived.

actions.description: Archive a club (superadmin only).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club archive. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club to archive. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

businessErrors.meaning: The requested new owner is missing or does not have an active membership in the club.
businessErrors.recovery: Create or reactivate the membership first, then retry the ownership transfer.

actions.description: Assign a new owner to a club (superadmin only).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this owner assignment. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club to reassign. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

actions.description: Create a new club (superadmin only).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club creation.

memberCap.description: Explicit member cap. Required when usesFreeAllowance is false. Must fit a Postgres integer.

ownerMemberId.description: Member ID of the club owner

usesFreeAllowance.description: When true, this club still counts against the owner’s free-club allowance.

actions.description: Get detailed club information and AI budget usage.

clubId.description: Club to inspect

actions.description: List all clubs (superadmin only).

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 50.

actions.description: Physically remove an archived club after writing one restore archive row.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club removal.

clubId.description: Archived club to remove. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

confirmSlug.description: Exact current slug, used as a destructive-action confirmation.

businessErrors.meaning: The requested club was not found.
businessErrors.recovery: Use superadmin.clubs.list or superadmin.clubs.get to confirm the clubId.

actions.description: Set whether any club, including an archived club, appears in the public directory. Changes appear within roughly 60 seconds because the public directory is TTL-cached.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Repeated calls with the same listed value leave the same directory-listed state.

clubId.description: Club to list or hide. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

listed.description: Whether the club should appear in the public directory.

businessErrors.meaning: The club is archived and cannot be updated.
businessErrors.recovery: Restore the club before changing it.

actions.description: Update mutable fields on a club (superadmin only).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this club update.

clubId.description: Club to update. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

memberCap.description: Explicit member cap. Required when usesFreeAllowance is false. Must fit a Postgres integer.

name.description: New club name (cannot be empty if provided)

usesFreeAllowance.description: Set true to put the club back onto the free allowance; false to move it out.

actions.description: List content across all clubs with optional filters.

clubId.description: Optional club filter. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

actions.description: Get platform diagnostics and health status.

businessErrors.meaning: That email is already registered to another member.
businessErrors.recovery: Use a different email, or recover the existing member out-of-band.

actions.description: Create a new platform member with a bearer token (no club membership).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this member + bearer token creation. Plaintext tokens are never replayed. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

email.description: Private contact email for the new member

publicName.description: Display name for the new member

actions.description: Get detailed info for a single member.
List all members with summary info.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

actions.description: Permanently remove a member and the rows that should disappear with them.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this hard delete.

confirmPublicName.description: Exact current publicName, used as a destructive-action confirmation.

reason.description: Operator reason for the permanent delete.

businessErrors.meaning: The member already has an active membership in the club.
businessErrors.recovery: Read the existing membership instead of creating another one.

businessErrors.meaning: The member has an active application block in this club from a temporary decline block or prior removal/ban.
businessErrors.recovery: Use the superadmin reactivation path for historical memberships, wait for a temporary block to expire, or remove the block out of band before creating a fresh membership.

actions.description: Add an existing member to a club (bypasses club admin requirement).

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this membership creation. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club to add the member to. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

initialStatus.description: Initial membership status (always active in the reference implementation)

actions.description: Read a DM thread newest-first as superadmin.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

actions.description: List DM threads across the platform. DMs are not club-scoped; each thread shows currently shared clubs between participants.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

actions.description: Register a producer, issue its initial secret, and register its initial topics.

burstLimit.description: Optional burst cap applied to this producer.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this producer secret mint. Plaintext secrets are never replayed. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

dailyLimit.description: Optional daily cap applied to this producer.

hourlyLimit.description: Optional hourly cap applied to this producer.

namespacePrefix.description: Required topic prefix for this producer. Use empty string only for core-like internal producers.

producerId.description: Stable producer identifier used in headers and registry rows.

topics.description: Initial registered topics owned by this producer.

deliveryClass.description: Delivery class used for rate limiting and operator policy.

topic.description: Registered topic owned by the producer.

actions.description: Rotate a producer secret with dual-secret overlap.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this producer secret rotation. Plaintext secrets are never replayed. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

producerId.description: Producer whose secret should be rotated.

actions.description: Enable or disable a producer globally.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Producer status updates set one status value; repeating the same status leaves the same producer state.

actions.description: Enable or disable a single producer topic.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Producer topic status updates set one status value; repeating the same status leaves the same topic state.

actions.description: Get platform-wide overview statistics.
List archived removed-club snapshots that may still be restorable.

clubSlug.description: Optional slug filter for one removed club lineage.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

actions.description: Restore a previously removed club from its archived payload.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this restore.

actions.description: Acknowledge notifications or mark a DM thread read.

idempotencyStrategy.reason: Acknowledging the same notification IDs or thread repeatedly leaves the same acknowledged/read state after the first call.

scopeRules: Use target.kind=notification to acknowledge notifications from the personal queue.
Use target.kind=thread to mark a DM thread read without replying.

actions.description: Preferred one-call polling surface for activity, notifications, and DM inbox summaries.

cursor.description: Opaque activity cursor from a previous response, or "latest" to seed from the current activity tip. Reuse the returned cursor even when hasMore is false.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

clubId.description: Optional club scope for the activity slice only. Notifications and inbox remain personal. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

unreadOnly.description: Only show threads with unread messages. Defaults to false on updates.list.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

notes: Use this for the general "has anything happened?" poll path.
The activity slice supports after="latest" to seed from the current activity tip.
Activity nextCursor is a stable resume cursor: when hasMore is false, keep that cursor and poll again later instead of discarding it.
The inbox slice defaults to unreadOnly=false so the DM part answers "show my DM inbox" by default; pass unreadOnly=true for unread-only triage.

nextCursor.description: Stable activity resume cursor. Always present; reuse it on the next poll even when hasMore is false.

seq.description: Opaque resume cursor for the club activity stream. Gaps are expected because audience filtering happens after the global sequence is assigned.
Opaque resume cursor for the member notification stream. Gaps are expected because the global sequence is assigned before any recipient-specific filtering.

businessErrors.meaning: The requested member is not vouchable in the specified club.
businessErrors.recovery: Choose a member who shares that club with the caller and is not the caller themselves, then retry.

businessErrors.meaning: The actor has already vouched for this member in this club.
businessErrors.recovery: Treat the existing vouch as canonical instead of retrying.

businessErrors.meaning: The submission was rejected for being too generic or lacking firsthand detail.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the feedback to the user, add one specific thing the voucher personally saw, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The submission was rejected for soliciting or facilitating clearly illegal activity.
businessErrors.recovery: Relay the reason to the user, revise the vouch text, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate returned a non-passing verdict after schema validation.
businessErrors.recovery: Review the feedback, revise the vouch text, and resubmit.

businessErrors.meaning: The content gate is temporarily unavailable.
businessErrors.recovery: Retry after a short delay. If the problem persists, surface the outage to the user.

actions.description: Vouch for another member in a club.

clientKey.description: Idempotency key for this vouch creation. Any non-empty string you generate. Scope it per-caller per-call. Retrying with the same key and the same payload replays the stored response.

clubId.description: Club context for the vouch. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships; scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

actions.description: List vouches for a member.

clubId.description: Optional club filter for vouches. Use the stable clubId from session.getContext.activeMemberships when you want one club; omit it to work across all accessible clubs. Scoped and post-membership surfaces take clubId, not clubSlug.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from previous response. Omit or null for first page.

limit.description: Max results. Must be between 1 and 20.

memberId.description: Member that is the subject of the vouches — i.e. vouches received (defaults to the calling member). Each result row already carries the creator as fromMemberId.

transport.acknowledgment: Acknowledge queued notifications via updates.acknowledge(notificationIds) and DM inbox entries via updates.acknowledge(threadId). Sending a DM reply also auto-marks that thread read for the sender. Activity advances via the activity cursor.

seq.description: Opaque resume cursor for the member notification stream. Gaps are expected because the global sequence is assigned before any recipient-specific filtering.
Opaque resume cursor for the club activity stream. Gaps are expected because audience filtering happens after the global sequence is assigned.
Opaque resume cursor for the member notification stream. Gaps are expected because the global sequence is assigned before any recipient-specific filtering.

stream.note: Browser EventSource cannot set Authorization headers; use fetch with a streaming reader.

after.description: Optional stream cursor seed. Use "latest" for the current activity/DM tips, or the composite streamCursor from a ready/activity/message event.

limit.description: Max activity or DM items per poll cycle inside the stream (1-20).

resumeHeaders.Last-Event-ID: Composite stream cursor (`a<activitySeq>:i<inboxSeq>`) emitted as the SSE id on ready, activity, and message events. Legacy activity-only ids are accepted for one deploy cycle.

stream.sseIdBehavior: Ready, activity, and message frames carry a composite SSE id that resumes both activity and DM inbox slices. notifications_dirty is invalidation-only and does not advance the cursor.

transportErrorCodes.meaning: The client cached an older schema. Refetch /api/schema and /skill, then retry.
